mutators.name: appendInventoryAdjustment

params.name: adjustment
params.type: InventoryAdjustmentInput /* { id?: AdjustmentHandle; logStatus: string; occurredAt: Timestamp; measure: MeasureHandle; units: UnitHandle; kind: 'increment' | 'decrement' | 'reset'; value: Decimal } */

mutators.semantics: Appends one asset-log adjustment event; resets assign value while increments and decrements are ledger deltas, and the adjustment's stable ID is used as the ascending tie-breaker for equal occurredAt values.

mutators.name: createQuantity

params.name: draft
params.type: QuantityDraft /* { measure: MeasureHandle; value: Decimal; units: UnitHandle; storedLabel?: string; bundle: QuantityTypeHandle; createdAt?: Timestamp } */

mutators.semantics: Appends and persists an initial revisioned quantity snapshot and returns its handle; its observable label is its entity ID/handle, never draft.storedLabel.

mutators.name: appendQuantityRevision

params.name: snapshot
params.type: QuantityRevisionDraft /* { measure: MeasureHandle; value: Decimal; units: UnitHandle; storedLabel?: string; bundle: QuantityTypeHandle; createdAt: Timestamp } */

mutators.semantics: Appends an immutable quantity revision rather than overwriting a snapshot; concurrent revisions remain retained and the materialized current fields use deterministic LWW/HLC resolution after sync.

mutators.name: appendQuantityTypeRevision

params.name: snapshot
params.type: QuantityTypeRevisionDraft /* { description?: string; label?: string; newRevision?: boolean } */

mutators.semantics: Appends an immutable quantity-type revision; concurrent revisions are preserved and a deterministic LWW/HLC winner is materialized after replicas converge rather than relying on a serialized revision lock.

mutators.name: migrateQuantities

params.name: configuration
params.type: QuantityMigrationConfiguration /* { staticDefaults: Partial<QuantityDraft>; fieldMappings: Record<string, string>; validation: QuantityValidationRule[] } */

mutators.semantics: If source is iterable, appends one persisted quantity per row after static defaults and then mapped row values are applied; a validation violation raises MigrationSkipRowError and appends no quantity for that row, while non-iterable source returns [] and appends nothing.

projections.name: getInventory
projections.returns: InventorySummary[] /* { measure: MeasureHandle; units: UnitHandle; value: Decimal } */
projections.semantics: Returns one independent summary for every exact (measure, units) pair with eligible adjustments at or before asOf; only logs whose status is exactly 'done' participate, and from the latest reset timestamp onward events are processed by (occurredAt, adjustment ID ascending), with each reset assigning the running value.

projections.name: getQuantitySnapshot
projections.returns: QuantitySnapshot /* { id: QuantityHandle; label: string; measure: MeasureHandle; value: Decimal; units: UnitHandle; bundle: QuantityTypeHandle; createdAt: Timestamp; revisions: QuantityRevisionHandle[] } */
projections.semantics: Materializes the quantity revision winner at asOf, exposes the retained revision handles, and always sets label to the quantity entity ID rather than the separately stored label field.

projections.name: getQuantityTypeSnapshot
projections.returns: QuantityTypeSnapshot /* { id: QuantityTypeHandle; description?: string; label?: string; revisions: QuantityTypeRevisionHandle[] } */
projections.semantics: Materializes the deterministic converged quantity-type revision at asOf while retaining concurrent revision handles, so a losing concurrent update is not erased from history.

projections.name: renderQuantityPlainText
projections.semantics: Renders the quantity in plain_text mode from its as-of snapshot, then strips rendered markup, decodes entities, trims, and replaces every whitespace run with one space.

rationale: Inventory adjustments are append-only log events and inventory is an as-of projection: it filters non-done and future-dated records, partitions exact measure/unit pairs, and implements timestamp-inclusive reset replay with ID ordering. Quantity and quantity-type edits are immutable revision events with deterministic eventual LWW/HLC materialization while retaining competing history. Quantity creation, label behavior, migration precedence/skip behavior, and rendered plain-text normalization are exposed because the mined fixtures observe them directly.
